workingDeparture.notes: Assume RI is activated Friday morning before work. Friday evening starts from Hope around 17:00, targets Silver Sands around 19:00, then charges and continues only as far west as is comfortable by about 21:30.

riActivation.preferredTiming: Friday morning before work from Rhode Island
riActivation.fallbackTiming: Only if the morning activation fails; otherwise do not spend Friday evening on RI.
riActivation.notes: Current plan assumes RI is complete before the 17:00 Friday road departure from Hope.

parkStopPlanning.meaning: For broad planning, count two hours per non-overnight park stop to cover leaving the highway, setup, activation, teardown, and returning to the route.
parkStopPlanning.overnightParkException: If the park is also the campsite, treat the drive/setup separately and plan the activation after camp is set up.

roveCounting.multiParkRule: Multi-entity activations count once per unique POTA entity; a 2-fer counts as 2 entities.
roveCounting.eveningStrategy: Evening activations can intentionally count toward the current UTC rove day and reduce the next local day's required load.

vehicle.chargingNetworkRule: Prefer Tesla Superchargers only for planned charging stops; do not treat generic EV chargers as equivalent when judging feasibility.
vehicle.sleepingMode: Car camping in the back of the Tesla
vehicle.carCampingRule: Sleeping in the vehicle reduces setup needs but does not remove the need for a legal reserved campsite or explicit overnight permission; do not plan to sleep in ordinary day-use parking lots.
vehicle.notes: Account for roughly 30 minutes at Tesla Superchargers after each 2.5 hours of driving. Friday likely needs a charge after Silver Sands, with Milford, Fairfield, Darien, Stamford, Greenwich, Dobbs Ferry, and Tarrytown all appearing along the westbound line.

campArrival.reason: Arrive with enough daylight to set up camp before full dark.
campArrival.activationTiming: For camping nights, activate the overnight park after camp is set up if needed.

weekendPlanning.notes: Saturday and Sunday have no work window, so they can carry more driving and more activations than weekdays.

candidateHighRoveDay.status: current working plan: make DC the large rove day because of the dense n-fer opportunities

constraints.jurisdictionActivationGoal: Activate every state and Washington, DC, crossed on the route.
constraints.multiParkActivationPriority: Nice to have; do not optimize the route around 2/3/4-fers unless they are convenient.

dcActivation.timing: Handle DC on Sunday June 21 from the Foggy Bottom hotel, with an optional friend meetup if timing works.
dcActivation.notes: The friend activation is nice to have, not a route constraint. Saturday night is already in DC, so Sunday should use the local density and then continue south to the Fredericksburg hotel.

outreach.purpose: Contact common activators near short-listed corridor parks for practical access, parking, antenna, and operating vibe checks.
outreach.emailDraft: Hi <name>, I am planning a June 2026 POTA road trip from Rhode Island to Florida and noticed you have activated <park/reference>. I am considering it for a brief activation while passing through and would appreciate any practical advice: easy parking, good operating spots, antenna constraints, safety/access issues, and whether it is realistic for about an hour on site. Thanks and 73, Rob / N1RWJ

timelineDrafts.name: Fixed-anchor southbound plan
timelineDrafts.recommendation: Use Friday to bank RI and CT, charge after Silver Sands, then stop in the lower Hudson / southwest Connecticut corridor by about 21:30. Saturday covers NY, NJ, PA, DE, and MD before the Foggy Bottom hotel, with MD allowed to slide after 20:00 EDT if needed for Sunday UTC credit.
assumptions: RI is activated Friday morning before work.
Friday road departure is from Hope, RI around 17:00 local.
Hope to Silver Sands is about 1h47 by Google Maps at the Friday departure time, so a roughly 19:00 CT arrival is realistic.
After the Silver Sands activation, charge the Tesla and keep driving only until roughly 21:30 local.
Friday does not need to cover NY; Saturday can start with a lower-Hudson NY activation.
Saturday must cover NY, NJ, PA, DE, and MD before the Foggy Bottom hotel; if MD happens after 20:00 EDT, it counts toward the Sunday UTC day.
Sunday remains the large DC / NoVA rove day, ending at the booked Fredericksburg hotel.

days.area: Hope, RI to Silver Sands, then lower Hudson / southwest Connecticut
days.focus: RI in the morning, CT at Silver Sands after work, Tesla charge, then a modest westbound hotel by about 21:30.
days.notes: Tarrytown/Nyack is ideal if the post-charge drive feels fine; Stamford/White Plains are acceptable if fatigue or charging timing pushes back.

days.area: Lower Hudson / southwest Connecticut to Foggy Bottom
days.focus: Activate NY, NJ, PA, DE, and MD on the way to the fixed DC hotel.
days.notes: Current route candidate: Tallman for NY, Cheesequake for NJ, John Heinz NWR at Tinicum for PA, Fox Point for DE, Fort McHenry for MD, then Foggy Bottom. PA reference still needs POTA verification.

days.focus: Make this the large rove day using the DC density, then continue south to the booked Fredericksburg hotel.
days.notes: LBJ Grove 7-fer is the top verification item; Mason Neck and Prince William Forest are southbound add-ons if energy and timing allow.

days.focus: Workday southbound push to the booked Florence stop while covering NC/SC as needed.
days.notes: Because Monday has a 13:00-17:00 work block, park choices need to be low-detour and close to the I-95 line.

days.focus: Use Tuesday to reduce the southern schedule risk and decide whether GA is a daytime stop or an overnight/camp activation.
days.notes: Exact overnight remains open; choose it based on charging, campsite availability, and how much distance remains to Ocala.

days.focus: Continue south; reaching Ocala Wednesday is acceptable but not required.
days.notes: If Florida is not banked earlier, pick a reliable low-detour Florida activation before or near Ocala.

days.focus: Stay at mother's house in Ocala and protect the final arrival day.
days.notes: This can be the low-stress Florida buffer night. If already in Ocala Wednesday, Thursday becomes flexible.

days.area: Ocala to Singer Island / Riviera Beach
days.focus: Final arrival for Field Day weekend.
days.notes: Florida should ideally be complete before this day so arrival is not dependent on another park stop.

areaBudgets.notes: Working Friday 17:00-18:00 departure block. If RI is activated Friday morning, this can be a simple CT reposition or CT activation near the first overnight; otherwise add about 1 hour for an outbound RI activation.
High-density corridor where multi-entity stops and evening UTC-day strategy could help a 10-entity rove goal.
Likely weekend work. Needs traffic-aware park choices and charging paired with food or a nearby activation.
Second weekend positioning block. If Sunday DC works, handle the friend activation here and keep the rest of the week southbound.
Best handled as a buffer/workday block around the DC evening activation rather than a high-mileage day.
A tight weekday segment because it must fit around the 13:00-17:00 work window unless the evening runs late.
Likely the hardest southern block. SC and GA should use very low-detour parks or multi-entity opportunities if available.
Useful arrival-buffer segment if Thursday gets long; can also carry the first Florida activation.
Keep the final Florida activation close to the route or destination unless earlier days create real slack.

planningConclusions: The large rove day should remain Sunday June 21 in DC / NoVA, because the DC n-fer density is much stronger than the Friday/Saturday corridor.
Friday should assume RI is already complete, then run Hope -> Silver Sands -> Tesla charge -> lower Hudson / southwest Connecticut hotel, stopping by about 21:30.
Do not push to Meadowlands Friday if it makes the night too late; Tarrytown/Nyack is enough westward progress and avoids a Saturday NJ-to-NY double-back.
If Friday stops in Stamford or White Plains instead, Saturday remains possible but has about 20 extra minutes of mapped driving versus Tarrytown/Nyack.
Saturday must cover NY, NJ, PA, DE, and MD before DC. The current route candidate is Tallman, Cheesequake, John Heinz NWR at Tinicum, Fox Point, and Fort McHenry.
John Heinz NWR is the practical PA routing candidate near I-95, but its exact POTA reference must be verified before treating it as final.
If Saturday runs late, let Fort McHenry / MD happen after 20:00 EDT; that banks MD for the Sunday UTC day while still keeping the southbound route coherent.

firstWeekendPlan.utcNote: In June EDT, the UTC day changes at 20:00 local. Friday evening activations after 20:00 local count toward the next UTC day.

fridayNightAnchors.name: Tarrytown / Nyack, NY
fridayNightAnchors.role: best Friday night target if the post-Silver-Sands charge finishes with enough energy to drive until about 21:30
pros: Gets into NY without requiring a Friday night NY activation
Starts Saturday close to Tallman Mountain State Park for the NY activation
Avoids driving to NJ and then backtracking north for NY
Tarrytown and Dobbs Ferry have Tesla Supercharger options along the route
cons: Requires Silver Sands, charging, and nearly another hour of westbound driving Friday night
If Silver Sands or charging runs long, it may push past the 21:30 comfort limit
fridayNightAnchors.saturdayImpact: Best current balance. Tarrytown/Nyack -> Tallman -> Cheesequake -> John Heinz NWR -> Fox Point -> Fort McHenry -> Foggy Bottom is about 5h35-5h37 mapped driving before activations, charging, food, and buffer.

fridayNightAnchors.name: White Plains / Stamford, NY-CT line
fridayNightAnchors.role: fatigue-aware fallback if the Friday charge makes Tarrytown/Nyack too late
pros: Keeps Friday within the 21:30 stop target more comfortably
Still starts Saturday within roughly 30 minutes of Tallman
Has multiple Tesla Supercharger options nearby or on approach
cons: Adds roughly 20 minutes to the Saturday NY-to-DC activation chain compared with Tarrytown/Nyack
Does not get quite as much of the lower Hudson crossing done Friday night
fridayNightAnchors.saturdayImpact: Still viable. Stamford -> Tallman -> Cheesequake -> John Heinz NWR -> Fox Point -> Fort McHenry -> Foggy Bottom is about 5h56 mapped driving before stops.

fridayNightAnchors.name: Milford / Fairfield, CT
fridayNightAnchors.role: short Friday fallback if the CT activation or charging consumes the evening
pros: Lowest-risk Friday night after the Silver Sands activation
Milford and Fairfield have Tesla Superchargers close to the route
Keeps Friday simple if traffic, weather, or fatigue is worse than expected
cons: Leaves too much for Saturday: NY, NJ, PA, DE, MD, and DC all remain ahead
Saturday becomes more fragile if any activation or charging stop runs long
fridayNightAnchors.saturdayImpact: Use only as a fatigue/safety fallback. It likely forces an earlier Saturday start.

saturdayRove.strategy: Saturday June 20 covers the remaining pre-DC jurisdictions from a lower-Hudson or southwest-CT hotel: NY, NJ, PA, DE, and MD. MD can be after the 20:00 EDT UTC rollover if needed, which would count it toward the Sunday UTC rove day. The main rove-count push remains Sunday in DC / NoVA.

timingAssumptions.source: Google Maps MCP route checks from Hope to Silver Sands, Friday post-activation hotel anchors, and Saturday lower-Hudson-to-DC chains; park hours and exact POTA references still need confirmation.
timingAssumptions.fridayActivationTiming: RI is assumed complete Friday morning. Silver Sands around 19:00 should be workable, with the official after-hours fishing parking as a useful access detail if the operation reaches the sunset boundary.
timingAssumptions.saturdayEveningTiming: If Fort McHenry / MD happens after 20:00 EDT Saturday, it counts toward the Sunday UTC rove day and reduces Sunday pressure.
timingAssumptions.chargingStopsIncluded: Friday likely needs a 30-minute Tesla Supercharger stop after Silver Sands. Saturday likely needs one or two additional 30-minute Supercharger stops depending on starting charge and hotel charging.
timingAssumptions.accessNotes: Silver Sands is listed as 08:00-sunset, and CT Parks notes after-hours fishing parking. Tallman is dawn-to-dusk, so it is a Saturday morning NY stop, not a Friday night solution. John Heinz NWR trails are sunrise-to-sundown, so it needs to be a daytime PA stop.

timingModels.estimatedTotalLabel: about 12h 35m from Saturday departure before any extra DC night activation

fridayEveningOptions.name: Recommended Friday: Hope -> Silver Sands -> Milford charge -> Tarrytown/Nyack hotel
fridayEveningOptions.route: Hope -> Silver Sands -> Tesla Supercharger near Milford/Fairfield -> Tarrytown or Nyack
fridayEveningOptions.estimatedTotalLabel: about 5h from a 17:00 departure, including a 30-minute charge but excluding dinner variation
fridayEveningOptions.comfort: best if the post-charge drive still feels good
fridayEveningOptions.notes: Hope to Silver Sands is about 1h47. Silver Sands to Milford Supercharger to Tarrytown is about 1h07; to Nyack is about 1h11. This fits a 21:30-ish stop only if the activation and charge stay tight.

fridayEveningOptions.name: Fallback Friday: Hope -> Silver Sands -> charge -> Stamford/White Plains hotel
fridayEveningOptions.route: Hope -> Silver Sands -> Tesla Supercharger near Milford/Fairfield/Darien -> Stamford or White Plains
fridayEveningOptions.estimatedTotalLabel: about 4h45-5h from a 17:00 departure depending on charging location
fridayEveningOptions.notes: Stamford is about 46 minutes from Silver Sands via the Milford charger; White Plains is about 60 minutes. This preserves Saturday feasibility without forcing a too-late Friday.

saturdayRove.restOfTripComfortRule: Saturday should arrive at the Foggy Bottom hotel with enough margin that Sunday can be the large DC rove day. Do not chase extra Saturday entities if it risks a late, exhausted DC arrival.

saturdayOvernight.reason: Saturday is now primarily a positioning day to the fixed DC-area hotel, not a maximize-count rove day.

openQuestions: Verify the John Heinz NWR at Tinicum POTA reference and whether a simple parking-lot / trailhead operation is valid and practical.
Pick the Friday hotel area after checking availability: Tarrytown/Nyack preferred, Stamford/White Plains fallback, Milford/Fairfield only as a safety fallback.
Confirm exact Fort McHenry operating position and whether it reliably counts US-0928 plus the relevant trail references.
Identify Saturday Tesla Supercharger pairings around central NJ / Philadelphia and Delaware / Baltimore.
Find a legal Saturday after-20:00 DC-area activation close to Foggy Bottom only if Saturday arrives early enough.

sundayDcRove.strategy: Use Sunday June 21 for the true dense rove from the Foggy Bottom hotel. Start with the LBJ Memorial Grove overlap in DC, then add nearby or southbound NoVA entities only if they fit the fixed Fredericksburg hotel night.

Find the best legal post-20:00 Saturday DC-area activation near Foggy Bottom to bank one Sunday UTC entity early.

campingPolicyNotes.currentRead: Minimum-stay rules are not uniform. Car camping in the Tesla helps with setup time and site-size needs, but most public parks still require an actual campsite reservation or explicit overnight permission.
campingPolicyNotes.carCampingRead: Less-developed areas are not a workaround in Connecticut unless they are designated for overnight use. CT backpack campsites are one-night options, but they are for true backpackers, dispersed camping is not permitted, and they do not solve vehicle-adjacent sleeping. Highway service plazas/rest areas may be viable rest/sleep stops in the vehicle, but they are not POTA park camping and should be treated as safety rest rather than a campsite.

jurisdictions.summary: CT state park campsites generally require two-night advance reservations, with one-night campsite reservations only inside the 1-3 day arrival window if available. Day-use parks generally close at sunset.
At least the relevant Harriman/Beaver Pond camping brochure says campsite reservations are minimum two nights; day-of arrivals may register subject to availability.
Maryland requires two-night weekend-linked stays from Memorial Day weekend through Labor Day, with three-night holiday-weekend rules.

North Carolina has a two-night minimum between Friday and Sunday on holiday weekends from Memorial Day through Labor Day, but the rule is waived for bookings within 7 days of arrival; some walk-up/onsite options exist.

jurisdictions.risk: lower for campsites, higher for late arrival logistics
jurisdictions.summary: Florida reservation information lists maximum stay rules and cabin two-night minimums, but not a general campsite minimum in the checked source. Late arrivals after sunset require calling the park before 5 p.m. for gate access.
Need park-specific reservation checks before relying on one-night stays. Do not assume the CT rule applies everywhere, but also do not assume one-night stays are available on summer weekends.

nferEvidence.sourceDescription: Candidate n-fer locations inferred from submissions by top activators.
nferEvidence.currentRead: Useful for finding proven multi-reference positions, but treat it as evidence to verify rather than proof that an activation will work from any exact parking spot.

saturday.summary: The data does not validate the assumed Gateway/Fort Wadsworth 2-fer.
saturday.notes: The current Saturday stretch should not rely on this pair until activator outreach or another source confirms it.

saturday.summary: Gateway National Recreation Area plus New Jersey Coastal State Trail appears as a data-backed 2-fer.
saturday.routingNote: Google Maps routing from Palisades to Sandy Hook Gateway to Cheesequake is about 2h total drive time, so this may be too expensive for the Saturday UTC target unless it replaces the Staten Island idea and the day is running early.

sunday.summary: The February 2026 CWops Solid Copy article describes an exact LBJ Memorial Grove position in DC that counts as a 7-fer.
sunday.routingNote: Foggy Bottom to LBJ Grove should be a short local hop; the nearby parking-lot/picnic-table option is described as a 4-fer if the exact 7-fer walk-out spot is not practical.

sunday.summary: Fox Point State Park plus Northern Delaware Greenway National Recreation Trail is a Delaware 2-fer candidate near I-95.
Fort McHenry has repeated n-fer evidence with Star-Spangled Banner and Captain John Smith Chesapeake trails.
sunday.routingNote: Central NJ to Fox Point to Fort McHenry to Rock Creek to Mason Neck is about 4h31m of driving before activation time.

sunday.summary: Mason Neck State Park plus Elizabeth Hartwell Mason Neck NWR is a Virginia 2-fer candidate for the Sunday camp concept.
sunday.routingNote: Rock Creek Park to Mason Neck State Park is roughly 55-63 minutes depending on the exact route.

assumptions: Friday road departure is from Hope, RI around 17:00 after RI has been activated in the morning.
Sunday night is fixed at a hotel in Fredericksburg, VA.
Monday June 22 is fixed in Florence, SC.
Thursday night is planned at mother's house in Ocala, FL; arriving Wednesday is acceptable but not required.
Broad planning uses 2 hours per non-overnight park stop, including detour, setup, activation, teardown, and return to the highway.
Weekdays still have a 13:00-17:00 work block unless explicitly changed.
Camping remains useful when it replaces a separate park stop, but the booked hotel/home anchors now drive the timeline.

overnightStrategy.goal: Use the booked hotels/home as the spine, then add camping only where it helps cover a jurisdiction without breaking the drive/work cadence.
recommendedPattern: 2026-06-19 hotel in lower Hudson / southwest CT
2026-06-23 TBD camp/hotel GA or north FL
2026-06-24 TBD camp/hotel or Ocala if early
overnightStrategy.summary: The immediate open booking decision is Friday night after Silver Sands and charging. Tarrytown/Nyack is preferred if it fits the 21:30 stop target; Stamford/White Plains is the fatigue-aware fallback. Saturday then covers NY/NJ/PA/DE/MD before Foggy Bottom.

nights.area: Tarrytown/Nyack preferred; Stamford/White Plains fallback; Milford/Fairfield safety fallback
nights.why: Friday should bank RI and CT without forcing a too-late push to Meadowlands. A lower-Hudson or southwest-CT hotel keeps Saturday coherent without a NJ-to-NY backtrack.
Booked/selected Saturday anchor. Arrive with enough margin for a possible post-20:00 DC activation.
Booked Sunday night after the DC/NoVA rove day.
Fixed Monday anchor. Use it to keep the southern route on schedule after the workday.
Open planning slot. A POTA campsite could cover GA or set up Florida, but a hotel may be smarter if Tuesday becomes a long workday drive.
Open buffer. Reaching Ocala Wednesday is fine; otherwise use this night to finish GA/FL without rushing Thursday.
Mother's house is the planned Thursday night anchor and protects the Friday Field Day arrival.

overnightStrategy.fallbackNote: If Tuesday/Wednesday camping is not practical, use hotels and add back a low-detour GA or FL daytime park stop.

days.focus: Use the DC / NoVA density for the large rove day, then finish at the booked Fredericksburg hotel.
days.notes: A Saturday post-20:00 DC activation can bank one Sunday UTC entity early. Sunday then prioritizes LBJ Grove, possible friend meetup, and southbound VA add-ons only while they fit Fredericksburg.

days.focus: Workday southbound push; cover NC and/or SC with low-detour stops while preserving the Florence arrival.
days.notes: This is likely a hard day with the 13:00-17:00 work block, so exact NC/SC parks need routing before booking anything else.

days.focus: Use Tuesday to cover any remaining SC and get GA handled or set up for a GA overnight activation.
days.notes: This is the next overnight to solve after Friday. A POTA campsite could be valuable if it covers GA without a separate daytime stop.

days.focus: Continue south, finish GA/FL coverage, and optionally reach Ocala early.
days.notes: If Ocala is reached Wednesday, Thursday becomes flexible. If not, keep the overnight close enough that Thursday is easy.

days.focus: Stay at mother's house and protect the final arrival day.
days.notes: If Florida still is not activated, use a nearby low-risk park before settling in.

days.focus: Destination day and hotel arrival for Field Day weekend.
days.notes: Keep this as an arrival day, not a required activation day.

restOfTripDraft.risk: The immediate planning risk is Friday night positioning. If Friday stops too far east, Saturday has too much to do before DC; if Friday pushes too far south without NY, Saturday creates a backtrack. Secaucus / Meadowlands is the current balance point.

overnight.name: TBD - Tarrytown/Nyack preferred, Stamford/White Plains fallback

booking.notes: Stop by about 21:30. Do not force Meadowlands Friday night.

days.notes: Activate RI Friday morning. Leave Hope around 17:00, activate Silver Sands around 19:00, charge, then drive west only as far as is comfortable.
Cover NY, NJ, PA, DE, and MD before the Foggy Bottom hotel. MD can slide after 20:00 EDT if needed for Sunday UTC credit.
Large DC / NoVA rove day, then continue south to Fredericksburg.
Workday southbound push; exact NC/SC parks still need routing.
Ocala Thursday anchor, or Wednesday if the trip runs early.
